"user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44991", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 5.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:N/VI:L/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-862", "name": "Missing Authorization"}], "description": "## Impact

OpenClaw deployments before `2026.4.21` could treat a non-owner sender as authorized for owner-enforced slash commands when all of the following were true:

- a channel plugin declared `commands.enforceOwnerForCommands: true`;
- the channel accepted wildcard inbound senders with `allowFrom: ["*"]`;
- no explicit `commands.ownerAllowFrom` was configured.

In that state, `src/auto-reply/command-auth.ts` reused the channel inbound wildcard as part of the command-owner decision. A sender who was not the owner could therefore pass the owner-command gate for commands such as `/send`, `/config`, or `/debug` on the affected channel.

The issue is limited to the command-owner authorization axis. It does not by itself grant owner-only tool access, host/sandbox access, or gateway administrator scope.

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` on npm
- Affected versions: `<= 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.21`

The latest public release, `2026.4.21`, contains the fix.

## Patches

The fix requires a concrete owner identity or internal operator-admin scope when a plugin enforces owner-only commands. Wildcard channel `allowFrom` no longer implies wildcard command ownership.

Fix commits:

- `2aa93d44a1b2c7058c371f261fda2b5d4de4a882` on `main`
- `995febb7b1e811ff6a1df5b18c22de94103f4c9f` in the `2026.4.21` release line

## Workarounds

Upgrade to `openclaw@2026.4.21` or later. Before upgrading, avoid wildcard/open-DM sender policy on owner-enforced channels, or configure `commands.ownerAllowFrom` to the intended owner identities.

## Credits

OpenClaw thanks @zsxsoft for reporting.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00237, "percentile": 0.14502}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-c28g-vh7m-fm7v", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-29T21:27:05Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-c28g-vh7m-fm7v", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-c28g-vh7m-fm7v"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44991"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-04-29T21:27:05Z", "references": ["https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-c28g-vh7m-fm7v", "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/2aa93d44a1b2c7058c371f261fda2b5d4de4a882", "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/995febb7b1e811ff6a1df5b18c22de94103f4c9f", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44991", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/openclaw-authorization-bypass-in-owner-enforced-commands-via-wildcard-channel-senders", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-c28g-vh7m-fm7v"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/security-advisories/GHSA-c28g-vh7m-fm7v", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw", "summary": "OpenClaw: Owner-enforced commands could accept wildcard channel senders as command owners", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-29T03:47:53Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-c28g-vh7m-fm7v", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2026.4.21", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "openclaw"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 2026.4.20"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: OpenClaw: Owner-enforced commands could accept wildcard channel senders as command owners
